items.headline: Nine new homes approved in Old Trace Road subdivision
items.summary: The City Council approved a plan to subdivide a vacant lot into nine single-family homes plus seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. This represents a significant increase in residential density for the Residential Estate zone.

items.headline: Summerhill redevelopment at El Camino Real moves forward
items.summary: Tree removal and replacement permits issued for the Palo Alto Greens HOA property associated with Summerhill's major redevelopment project at 4345 El Camino Real. This signals progress on a significant housing/mixed-use development that residents have been tracking.

items.headline: City approves lease bonds for new public safety building
items.summary: Mountain View City Council approved lease revenue bonds to finance a new public safety building project. This represents a significant capital investment in city infrastructure that will affect long-term municipal finances.

items.headline: Council reviews biodiversity and urban forest plan for Mountain View
items.summary: The City Council held a study session to review a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan that will shape how the city manages green spaces and environmental quality. This plan could affect local policies on tree preservation, landscaping, and habitat protection.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers 67-unit mixed-use development with retail
items.summary: A 4.22-acre site would be redeveloped with 55 townhomes, 12 apartment units, and 18,535 square feet of retail space, including a preserved Speedy's Tacos location. The project requires a special development permit and would subdivide the land into 17 lots for what appears to be a significant infill development in the city.

items.headline: New Dental Office Opens at The Villages
items.summary: Bay Hill Dentistry is opening a new dental office at 2933 The Villages Parkway with a $629k investment. This adds healthcare services to the popular residential community in South San José.

items.headline: Campbell revises business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council considered an ordinance to update business license tax rates in the municipal code. This annual adjustment affects local businesses operating in Campbell.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara approves temporary giant signs near Levi's Stadium
items.summary: The City Council authorized temporary supergraphic signs on building surfaces around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. This is a notable visual change to the area for the limited duration of the tournament.
